thread.title: General Assembly adopts resolution on Palestinians’ right to “self-determination”.
thread.title_full: General Assembly adopts resolution on Palestinians’ right to “self-determination”.

author: archyworldyscom
title: General Assembly adopts resolution on Palestinians’ right to “self-determination”.
text:
Adopted A resolution was passed by an overwhelming majority to recognize the right of the Palestinian people to self-determination on their land and to obtain independence, freedom and liberation from Israeli occupation , without delay.

The Palestine Information Center said: “The decision affirms that the right of the Palestinian people to self-determination on their land is considered an inalienable right and is not subject to any conditions or reservations, nor is it acceptable for bargaining or negotiation, nor is it subject to justifications presented under the guise of security measures that continue to be used by the Israeli occupation.”

The Center reported that “the United Nations General Assembly, through its Committee on Human Rights and Humanitarian Affairs, stated that the resolution refers to the Advisory Opinion of the International Court of Justice regarding the illegality of the Israeli occupation and the need to put an immediate end to it, after the occupation has constituted a serious obstacle for the Palestinian people in the exercise of their right to self-determination and the creation of their independent state”.

Interestingly, the resolution “received the support of 170 countries, including Canada, Australia, European Union countries and all the countries of South America, as well as many Asian and African countries, while only six countries opposed the resolution: Israel, United States, Argentina, Paraguay, Micronesia and Naru.

Interestingly, on May 10, 2024, the United Nations General Assembly approved the decision to join Palestine as a state in the United Nations.

 Two prisoners from Nablus and Gaza have died in Israeli prisons

The Prisoners and Ex-Prisoners Affairs Authority and the Palestinian Prisoners Club were informed of the death of two prisoners in Israeli prisons, Samih Suleiman Muhammad Aliwi (61 years old) from Nablus and Anwar Shaaban Muhammad Aslim (44 years old) from Gaza.

The Prisoners’ Commission and the Prisoners’ Club held Israel fully responsible for the deaths of the two prisoners, Aliwi and Aslam.

The Commission and the Club renewed their “call on the international human rights system to put an end” to the horrible state of impotence that is damaging its role in the face of genocidal war crimes and crimes committed against prisoners and detainees in prisons of the Israeli occupation. .

The Palestinian Ministry of Health in the Gaza Strip announced that the death toll from the ongoing Israeli shelling of the Strip has risen to 43,764 dead and 103,490 injured as of October 7, 2023.





 Suggest a correction

organizations.name: Advisory Opinion of the International Court of Justice
